description: A slow client's outbound messages queue without limit, exhausting server memory and taking down all connections.
body: Failure mode: the server enqueues every message for a connection whose socket write is slow, so the per-connection buffer grows until the process runs out of memory and all clients drop. Detection signals: memory grows with the slowest client's lag; no high-water mark or drop policy on the send queue; OOM kills correlate with bursty broadcasts. Do not buffer outbound messages without bound. Safer fallback: bound the outbound queue, coalesce to latest-state or disconnect laggards with a resync contract, and load-test that memory stays flat under a slow consumer.
